title: holcus sorghum var. caffrorum
intro: Holcus sorghum var. caffrorum, also known as wild sorghum, is a type of plant in the grass family Poaceae. It is commonly used in traditional medicine due to its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial activities. The plant is valued for its potential health benefits, such as reducing fever, relieving digestive issues, and treating skin conditions. In herbal preparations, Holcus sorghum var. caffrorum is typically dried and used as a tea or made into a decoction to take advantage of its medicinal properties.
